observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations, and Management agreed to direct the OCA and the CTO to continuously exert more efforts in obtaining the necessary documents to effect the adjustment of the remaining unreconciled items.

We also recommended, and Management agreed to direct:

The CTO to regularly coordinate with CCAH personnel in-charge on the receipt of ACPNs and submit to the OCA for timely recording in the books of accounts;

The CTO to process the CAFOA for the loan amortization to facilitate the recording of the ₱11.928 million disbursement; and

The Chief of Hospital of CCAH to provide the CTO a List of ACPNs received via    E-mail daily;

The CTO to fast track the retrieval of DVs and its supporting documents for the unrecorded disbursements of ₱36,136.39 for the GF-Proper; and

The OCA to furnish COA with copies of JEV for the recognition of the noted unrecorded DM and CMs in the accounting books for our evaluation.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to:

Submit to COA a listing of the status of the employees’ compliance to their respective disallowances/ charges to support the figures reported in the year-end FS under the Receivables – Disallowance/ Charges account; and

Continuously and fast track the retrieval of source documents to facilitate the reconciliation of the account; and

Prepare and maintain SL for each liable person.

We reiterated our recommendations, and Management agreed to direct:

CEO, as well as the other concerned implementing offices, to submit to OCA the necessary documents of fully implemented and/or completed PPAs to facilitate the adjustments of the affected accounts.

The OCA to:

Continuously perform analysis, verification and confirmation from the CEO and other concerned implementing offices of the non-moving PPAs if these were already fully implemented and/or still on-going based on the Status Report of Implementation of PPAs;

Facilitate the adjustments of affected accounts through regular reconciliation of records on fully implemented and/or completed PPAs;

Confirm the unexpended/unutilized balances of fund transfers with the SA and return the same upon demand of the latter.  Otherwise, unused balance shall be returned to the Bureau of the Treasury; and

Intensify efforts on the retrieval of records and/or supporting documents of Item No. 21 (Various Beginning balances of Prior Years' Projects) amounting to ₱110.869 million under Due to NGAs account and prepare SL to substantiate the amount recorded in the books of accounts.

We also recommended, and the CPDC agreed to fast track the necessary documentation on the ROW of Item No. 22 (Construction of Dipolog River Park, 120 Linear Meter-down Stream) under Due to NGAs account and Item No. 9 (Construction of Miputak-Sta. Isabel-Estaka Bypass Road with Drainage Component) of Due to LGUs account, and submit the required papers for liquidation to the OCA.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to process the request for write-off of the unliquidated Cash Advances of the deceased Accountable Officers of the city.

We also reiterated our recommendations with which Management agreed to direct the: 

City Accountant to continue sending demand letters to the concerned personnel with unliquidated Cash Advances requiring the liquidations of Cash Advances and effect the necessary sanctions for non-liquidation of the same; and

CHRMO to gather and provide the OCA with information on the known addresses of the AOs that are no longer in service to facilitate the sending of demand letters to all AOs with unliquidated balances.

We recommended, and Management agreed to: 

Direct the LFC to:

Propose for the update and amendment of the Local Revenue Code.

Ensure that the projected / expected income are reasonable, realistic, and are collectible in the ensuring year.

Review the PPAs under continuing appropriations (Capital Outlay) and recommend for adjustments / reversal of appropriations of PPAs to match with the cash receipts during the year.

Review the obligations made as of yearend of 2023 and ensure that the obligations are covered only by cash receipts during the year.

Direct the City Treasurer to:

Inform the OCB of the status of cash receipts as of given period prior to issuance of subsequent ARO.

Determine doable measures to improve collection efficiency of internally-sourced taxes, fees and charges and initiate tax campaign to compel delinquent taxpayers to pay taxes due provided in the Local Revenue Code and Section 256 of RA No. 7160.

Examine the accuracy of the declared gross receipts / sales of taxpayers by considering all available evidence upon which the business tax is based to come up with the correct assessment.

Conduct examination of books of accounts of the business taxpayers after payment period for reassessment of the business taxes and require payments thereof for under assessment.

Issue demand letters to delinquent business taxpayers and ensure that business permits are not renewed with unpaid business taxes.

Direct the OCB to properly monitor the appropriations and coordinate with the City Treasurer on the cash availability prior to issuance of ARO to the budget responsibility centers.

We reiterated our recommendations, and Management agreed to:

Ensure that identified development projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent DF are well-planned, relevant and essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people, and implementation-and procurement-ready;

Instruct the CPDC to carefully identify, thoroughly review and monitor PPAs and their budget allocations so that idle fund balances may be re-appropriated for other development projects in the AIP and AB for consideration of the LFC and evaluation and approval of the Local Development Council; and

Direct the CEO and CPDC to initiate the procurement of all projects chargeable to the 20 per cent DF in the first quarter of each year.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the City DRRM Officer to:

Coordinate with the CEO and expedite the implementation of PPAs under the yearly plan to optimize the available LDRMMF intended to support disaster risk management PPAs;

Provide specific PPAs and their corresponding appropriations and timelines in the LDRRMFIP; and

Closely monitor the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the implementation of PPAs is carried out within the allotted time frame.   For PPAs which are no longer feasible for implementation, propose appropriate amendments and/or revisions to the LDRRMFIP to reflect new or realigned PPAs.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

Submit written explanation to COA on the incurrence of cash deficit as discussed above;

Direct the LCF to closely monitor the utilization of funds and immediately adopt appropriate revenue measures to recover the cash deficit; and

Direct the City Treasurer to ensure that cash deficit incurred at year-end will be adequately covered with the first collections of the immediately succeeding fiscal year in accordance with Section 337 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

Strengthen its planning and budget review process to make them more effective partners in managing SEF towards upgrading of educational outcomes particularly in the implementation of infrastructure projects which should have been supported by POW to arrive at a more realistic budget to avoid inadequacy of funds leading to delay in the implementation of PPAs and excessive unexpended obligation of PPAs under SEF; 

Direct the CBO to revise the SAAOB reflecting therein the augmentation and reversion of ₱1.525 million and furnish COA copy of the same. Moving forward, intensify efforts in the monitoring and update of budget registries and review of schedules and documents supporting the figures in the SAAOB.

We also reiterated our recommendation and the CEO agreed to continuously fast-track the implementation of on-going and unimplemented infrastructure projects of the LSB.

We recommended that Management direct the OCB to review the submitted revised PPMPs of each of the Offices and submit the same to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) for consolidation to final APP in accordance with Section 7 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management direct the OCB and City Accountant to refrain from using the Security Services account for the budget allocation and recording of the wages of job order personnel in the AB and books of accounts, respectively, moving forward.

We recommended that Management direct all requisitioning offices to properly plan its procurement activities to ensure that PPAs are implemented and completed within the projected schedule. Also, observe timeliness for each procurement activity such as in the preparation and approval of procurement documents from invitation to bid, compliance with the requirements for each supporting document until the accomplishment of the AIR as stated in Section 5 of GPPB No. 06-2019 dated July 17, 2019.

We recommended that Management direct:

The BAC to ensure that thorough post-qualification procedures are being conducted, to include, among others, barring those key personnel and equipment which are already pledged/ used in other on-going projects; and

The CPDC and CEO to constantly monitor the simultaneous implementation of projects by the same contractor to guard against unwarranted delays or substandard quality of work.

Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Engineer to submit to COA the results of the test analysis of construction materials as mandated by DPWH DO No. 11 series of 2017 dated January 24, 2017.

Moving forward, we recommended, and management agreed to direct:

The Lead offices to provide realistic budget for each activity of each program or project of the City and ensure that these are undertaken within the target period; and

The GFPS Committee to lead the efficient and effective implementation of planned GAD activities in the GPB, install adequate monitoring controls for timely completion of GAD activities, and ensure that the utilization of the GAD fund is optimized.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Consider closing the books of the CPS after thorough analysis and reconciliation of its accounts including submission of billing documents by the CEO to OCA for proper accounting and recording in the books and transfer the reconciled accounts to their appropriate books such as the GFP, 20 per cent DF, TF and SEF, as the project costs were mostly sourced from these funds; and

 Direct the City Accountant and City Engineer to:

Submit a written plan of action, which includes among others the timeline for the cleansing of the accounts involved;

Review and reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary adjustments and furnish COA with copies of JEVs for further evaluation; and

Establish regular reconciliation of accounts in order to eliminate the above noted differences in the accounts and prevent the same from recurrence.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to direct the OCA and the Officer In-Charge of the CHRMDO) to:
	
Fast track the review and analysis of the reported balance reflected in the sub-accounts with negative balances; and

Effect proper adjustments thereof for the fair presentation of this account in the FS.

For Due to GSIS and Other Funds accounts, we reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to direct the OCA and OIC-HRMDO to:
	
Fast track the review and analysis of the reported balance reflected in the account;

Effect proper adjustments thereof for the fair presentation of this account in the FS; and

Coordinate with GSIS agency to confirm the adjusted amounts, resolve discrepancies, if any, and remit remaining withheld GSIS contributions.

Also, we reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to direct the OCA and OIC-HRMDO to:

Fast track the reconciliation of the Due to SSS sub-accounts under the Due to GOCCs account;

Effect proper adjustments thereof for fair presentation of this account in the FS; and

Coordinate with SSS to confirm the adjusted amounts, resolve discrepancies, if any, and remit remaining withheld SSS contributions.

For dues to HDMF, we reiterated our recommendations that Management direct the OCA and OIC-HRMDO to:

Fast track the review and analysis of the reported balance reflected in the Due to Pag-Ibig and Due to Other Funds accounts as well as its sub-accounts with negative balances;

Effect proper adjustments thereof for the fair presentation of these accounts in the FS; and

Coordinate with HDMF Office to confirm the adjusted amounts, resolve discrepancies, if any, and remit remaining withheld PIC.

Likewise, for dues to PHIC, we reiterated our recommendations that Management direct the OCA and OIC-HRMDO to:

Fast track the review and analysis of the reported balance reflected in the Due to PhilHealth and Due to Other Funds accounts;

Effect proper adjustments thereof for fair presentation of these accounts in the FS; and

Coordinate with PHIC Office to confirm the adjusted amounts, resolve discrepancies, if any, and remit remaining withheld PHC.

The CTO compute the RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of each year based on the total assessed value of taxable properties as reported in the Assessment Roll of the CAO at the end of the preceding year, duly supported by the RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s Index Card; and

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

Enhance internal control policies and procedures in the processing and monitoring of claims from PHIC to ensure that process owners are properly supervised and accountable to their outputs, reimbursable claims are submitted on time and reduce, if not eliminate, denied and returned claims due to non-compliance issues.

Direct the City Accountant to:

Set up Allowance for Impairment – Due from GOCCs account on the non-moving accounts of ₱22.160 million and Denied and RTH claims totaling ₱31.922 for fair presentation of the Due from GOCCs account in the FS; 

Provide details of set up of receivables and payments/reimbursements of Hospital and PF shares in the SL of the Due from GOCCs account based on the monthy report of receivables/statement of account and monitoring report of RTH and Denied Claims to include Notification Letters (RTH/Denied Claims) by the CCAH Billing and Claim Section;

Conduct monthly verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables; and

Coordinate with the CCAH Supervising Administrative Officer on the reconstruction of data on PHIC receivables including denied and RTH claims for CYs 2015 to 2017 to support the SL balances.

Direct the SAO of the CCAH to: 

Submit  to the City Accountant the following documents for proper recording of receivables and PHIC reimbursements and set up of impairment allowance on denied and RTH claims on a monthly basis:
Report of Receivables from PHIC to include supporting documents on the collections of HCI and PF payments/ reimbursements; and

Monitoring Reports on RTH and Direct Claims together with Notification Letters from PHIC on said claims.

Perform data reconstruction for claims covering the CYs 2015 to 2017.

We recommended, and Management agreed to: 

Improve policies on the acceptance of promissory notes for balances of hospital fees charged to patient, as well as enhance collection efforts on the outstanding receivables; and

Direct the OCA to set-up Allowance for Impairment – Accounts Receivable for fair presentation of the Accounts Receivable account in the FS.

Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to:

Exert prudence in the disbursement of the LDRRM and immediately stop the practice of charging monthly wages of LGU job hires and honorarium for contract service against the LDRRMF; and

Strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 10121, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-JMC No. 2013-1, and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM CSC JMC No. 2014-1 as to the utilization of the LDRRMF. Henceforth, ensure that expenses which are PS and administrative in nature shall be sourced out from the GF of the City.

Moving forward, we recommended that both the CDRRMO and the OCA to closely coordinate, reconcile, and monitor their efforts so that a correct and comprehensible LDRRMFIP is accomplished prior to submission to the Audit Team.

We reiterated our recommendation with which the Chairperson, Committee on Women, Children, Family Welfare and GAD, with the assistance of the City GFPS, agreed to formulate GAD Code for the LGU supported by in-depth study and/or analysis of the gender issues of the women, men and children of Dipolog City and submit the same to the Sanggunian for enactment into Ordinance.

We reiterated our recommendation with which the Chairperson, Committee on Women, Children, Family Welfare GAD to spearhead the passage of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313 to be assisted by the City GFPS and post the same in conspicuous places. Further, include in the ordinance provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

Inclusion of completed Road Networks amounting to ₱33.536 million in the Road Network account despite transfer to barangays, which is not in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Paragraph 82 of IPSAS 17 states that “The carrying amount of an item of PPE shall be derecognized:
xxx
On disposal; or
When no future economic benefits or service potential is expected from its use or disposal.”
Review on the movement of Road Networks during 2023 revealed that completed Road Network projects amounting to ₱33.536 million have been turned-over to and accepted by various barangays of this city, evidenced by a Certificate of Project Turn-over duly acknowledged by the corresponding incumbent Punong Barangay. Details are shown below:
Considering that responsibility on the said infrastructure has been transferred to the recipient barangay, the listed Road Networks should have been excluded in the Road Network at year-end. As a result, reported Road Network is overstated by the same amount.
During the exit conference, it was clarified that turn-over of projects regarding Road Network components are done when the Road Network involved is not part of the properties under the custody of the City.
Hence, the reported Road Network account is overstated by ₱33.536 million as of December 31, 2023.
Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The OCA to Coordinate with the CGSO, and other concerned offices on the details of the ₱33.536 million Road Networks that have already been turned over to barangays to facilitate adjustment in the accounting books; and
The CGSO to coordinate with the OCA on the Road Network projects that have been turned-over to barangay LGUs to facilitate exclusion from the property records.
Misclassification of incidental expenses for road lots acquisition to Land account amounting to ₱2.950 million
Paragraph 26 of the IPSAS 17 for PPE states that, “An item of PPE that qualifies for recognition as an asset shall be measured at its cost”. This includes all costs necessary to get the asset ready for its intended use. Also, the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs describes the account title for Land and Road Networks for proper classification of transactions in the books.
Review of the only available SL maintained under the TF for Land account revealed that a total of ₱2.950 million pertained to accumulated expenses incurred in the processing of ownership documents for road lots. Details are as follows:
An entry during 2023 that reduced the first listed SL revealed that reclassification to Road Network (1-07-03-010) was only done when final payment to the owners/sellers was made. Considering that these SLs were maintained for the specified road projects, the listed figures should have been part of Road Network for proper reporting. Hence, the Road Network account is understated by ₱2.950 million while the Land account is overstated by the same amount.
Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the OCA to prepare the JEV to reclassify the ₱2.950 million from the TF to be part of the road lot for Road Network.
Non-maintenance of PPELCs and PCs for some PPEs totaling ₱1.683 billion, and ₱2.228 billion, respectively, and existence of abnormal negative balance in the SL of Road Network in the amount of ₱2.230 million.
The COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 prescribes the Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of PPE, Recognition of PPE items Found at Station and Disposition of Non-existing/Missing PPE items, for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
Item 6.1.3 of the same Circular states that “The Accounting Unit shall update its record of acquisition/disposal/transfer of PPEs in the PPELCs and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger.”
On the other hand, the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 was released to provide accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System in LGU. Part VII of the circular enumerates the duties and responsibilities of each office in the recognition of the Road Network as part of the Infrastructure Assets of the LGU, as follows:
Local Accountant: xxx
3. 	Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; xxx
General Services Officer: xxx
2.	Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; xxx
A significant aspect of the reconciliation procedure is the existence of properly filled up and updated property and ledger cards for each class of PPE. The PPELCs are also expected to contain the details to support the figures reported in the FS as well as the guide in the preparation of the annual RPCPPE. As a result of the ongoing cleansing of PPE, the City was able to submit a year-end RPCPPE showing total Land (1-07-01-010) at ₱289.247 million that is reconciled with the reported figure in the FS. However, inspection at the OCA and the CGSO disclosed that such reported amount was not supported with PPELCs and PCs. This is a reiterated audit observation since the commencement of the city’s PPE cleansing activities last CY 2021.
On the other hand, as of December 31, 2023, the City’s FS reported total Road Network at ₱409.742 million, that is reconciled with the submitted Report of Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN). However, upon inspection of the local Road Network’s PCs, these were not fully updated to support the reported figures in the RPCLRN. As for the local Road Network ledger cards, the OCA has claimed that data has been entered into the current system, but was not available for viewing upon inspection due to technical issues. While there are available SLs, details only cover the increase during 2023 for the Road Network recorded under the 20 per cent (DF, which only amounted to ₱53.877 million. We also noted an abnormal balance in the SL that reduced the total reported amount by ₱2.230 million.
Moreover, some PPEs other than the Land and Road Network accounts mentioned above amounting to ₱1.683 billion do not have PPELCs, and PPEs totaling ₱2.228 billion do not have PCs. The absence of PPELCs and PCs affects the reliability of the PPE accounts presented in the FS as of yearend.
Based on the foregoing discussions, it can only be inferred that the City has yet to fully comply with the procedures and guidelines in cleansing the PPE balances as of December 31, 2023 as mandated under COA Circular 2020-006, which could have given the City the opportunity for one-time cleansing of its PPE accounts.
Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The OCA to:
Determine the nature of the abnormal negative balance of ₱2.230 million and prepare JEV to adjust the same;
Compile and finalize the PPELCs to completely support the reported value of the Land in the FS;
Compile and finalize the Road Network Ledger Cards to completely support the reported value of the Road Network in the FS;
Compile and finalize the PPELCs of Immovable Assets, other than Land and Road Networks accounts, to completely support the same PPE items reflected in the FS;
Expedite efforts in reaching out to system providers in order to fix the technical issues regarding the information for Road Networks.
The CGSO to:
Compile and finalize the PCs for Land to completely support the Land balance reflected in the RPCPPE;
Compile and update the local Road Network PCs to completely support the Road Network balance reflected in the RPCPPE;
Compile and finalize the PCs of Immovable Assets, other than Land and Road Networks accounts, to completely support the same PPE items reflected in the RPCPPE;
Update and complete the PCs of Movable Assets to support the same PPE items replicated in the RPCPPE; and
Expedite efforts in obtaining Road Network ID Numbers for complete property records.

Fast track the implementation of those PPAs which have not yet started and have remained unfinished as at year-end; and

Exert more efforts in their project monitoring system and   consistently observe and enhance the same so that PPAs are completed on time, hence, resulting in prompt delivery of socio-economic benefits to its constituents.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
1.10		We recommended that Management:
Require the CGSO to continue with the necessary actions for the remaining identified shortage in the Military, Police and Security Equipment and to report the same to the OCA to effect the adjustments in the books of accounts, as warranted;
Direct the OCA to compile and finalize the PPELCs to completely support the PPE account balances reflected in the FS; and
Submit its amended plan of action to the COA, incorporating the provisions in COA Circular No. 2020-006, including complete details on time periods and corresponding names of personnel delegated for each action/task for CY 2021.

Recommendations:
2.6 	We recommended that the OCA instruct the staff-in-charge to update and incorporate the additions to the RN to the corresponding LRNLCs to support the figures reflected in the financial reports.
2.7		We also recommended that Management:
Direct the OCA and the OCE to take the necessary actions to update the LRNLCs and the LRNPCs that were prepared by both offices to reflect a reconciled amount to support a consistent figure reflected in the FS as well as in the property records;
Direct the CGSO to coordinate with the OCE to verify whether the noted road lots included in the Land property records are separate and distinct from those declared under the road lot component of the RN and proceed with effecting the adjustments, as may be necessary; and
Assess and formulate internal control policies in the delegation of duties regarding custodial functions of property cards for the growing number of infrastructure assets of the City, particularly the RNs, with emphasis on the accountability of the CGSO who holds responsibility over buildings and other physical structures of the City, as embodied in the Local Government Code (LGC).

Recommendations:
We reiterated our recommendations that Management:
Direct the OCA and CGSO to continue with the reconciliation of the results of the physical count of inventories or RPCI with their respective records and immediately investigate, clear and reconcile all discrepancies between physical count and accounting and supply records. If necessary, written explanations are to be required from accountable and responsible persons;
Require the City Accountant to demand from persons responsible the submission of SSMI together with the original copy of the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) as basis in the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to credit the account for issued supplies, and correspondingly reflect correct balances of the Inventory Accounts in the books; and
Require the City Accountant to disclose all unreconciled accounts in the NFS.
3.7 	We also recommended the OCA prepare separate subsidiary ledgers (SL) for the current year purchases of FOL Inventory to allow for semi-annual reconciliation with the amount found during the count as evidenced by the RPCI submitted by the CGSO.
3.8 	Further, we recommended that Management direct the supply officers at both the CCAH and the CHO to submit to the Accounting Division the remaining SSMIs on all requisitions for 2020 and prior years, prior to the conduct of the semi-annual physical count. This will allow adjustments that will accurately reflect the balance of the Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory account and facilitate reconciliation between the RPCI and the Accounting Records.
3.9 	Lastly, we recommended that Management direct the CGSO and all the supply officers to submit SSMIs to the OCA for the succeeding periods on a monthly basis to match with the monthly preparation of the trial balance so as to allow regular recording of expenses and prevent the accumulation of balances that will hinder the reconciliation between the accounting records and the RPCI.

Recommendations:
4.20 	We reiterated our recommendations that Management:
Direct the OCT to submit a written explanation as to the whereabouts of the documents of the remaining unrecorded disbursements of ₱36,136.39 for the GF Proper and the ₱4,110.00 for the TF‘s–OBO, and submit the covering vouchers and supporting papers to the OCA, as basis for adjusting the reconciling items in the books.
Direct the OCT to provide the OCA with the details regarding the unrecorded debit and credit memoranda of the prior years or any other document that could facilitate the adjustment of the said items in the books.
Direct the OCT to regularly request snapshots of bank statements of all depository accounts of the City every month-end and use the same to facilitate updating of their records.
Direct the OCT to routinely furnish the requested bank account snapshots within an agreed number of days after month-end to the OCA to facilitate identification of reconciling items as well as the early preparation of the BRS.
Direct both the OCA and the OCT to exert more efforts in obtaining the necessary documents to effect the adjustment of the remaining unreconciled items.
Direct the OCA and OCT to continuously exert effort in tracing the origin, and determining the nature/status of the accounts with no known account numbers, and taking the necessary action to properly reflect the true condition of the ₱0.973 million CIB accounts in the books of the City.
4.21 	We also recommended the OCA to include a disclosure regarding the accounts with inexistent bank balances in the NFS.
4.22 	Lastly, in line with the commitment of both the OCA and the OCT to a periodic reconciliation, we recommend that this be done on a monthly basis to match with the preparation of BRS and to aid in the identification of material differences between their records, and arrive at a reliable CIB - Local Treasury, Current Account balance.

Recommendations:
5.12 	We reiterated our recommendations that the OCA continue:
Sending demand letters to the AOs concerned requiring the liquidation of CAs.  Effect the necessary sanctions for non-liquidation of the same; and
To monitor compliance with the requirements for the write-off of the unliquidated CAs of deceased officers and employees of the City and submit to the Office of the Auditor the corresponding Request for Write-off together with the said documents and requirements.
5.13 	We also recommended that the Management direct the CHRMDO to:
Inform the OCA of all payroll arrangements related to the settlement of CAs and to strictly monitor these arrangements; and
Gather and provide the OCA with information on the last known addresses of AOs who are no longer in service, to facilitate the sending of demand letters to all AOs with unliquidated balances.

Recommendations:
6.6 	We reiterated our recommendation that the OCA review and reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary adjustments.
6.7 	We also recommended that the Management consider the creation of a task force as the audit finding is recurring, and submit to COA a written plan of action on the reconciliation of the accounts involved.

Recommendations:
7.6	We recommended that the OCA:
Submit a listing of the status of the employees’ compliance with their respective disallowances/ charges to support the figures reported in the year-end FS under the Receivables – Disallowance/Charges account; and
b. 	Prepare and maintain SLs for the account in compliance with Section 114 (2) of PD 1445.
7.7	We also recommended that Management submit a written plan of action for the collection of the remaining balance of the Receivables – Disallowances/Charges account amounting to ₱3.851 million.

Recommendation:
8.9 	We reiterated our recommendation that the OCE as well as the other offices concerned identify the completed projects per record of their respective offices after which they supply the OCA with the necessary documents to facilitate the adjustment of the said accounts.

Recommendations:
9.9 	We recommended that the City Accountant:
Analyze the account and retrieve from available records on file the JEV and corresponding supporting documents to identify the nature of each transaction lodged in the account and effect journal entries, if warranted.  If documents are no longer available on file, request from COA the necessary documents being the repository of all documents pertaining to government financial transactions;
Prepare and submit to COA subsidiary records and supporting documents to substantiate the initial recording of P113.73 million in CY 2016 and year-end balance of P111.94 million; and
Provide additional information in the NFS to support the account balance and aid users in understanding the account as required in IPSAS 1 and 9.

Recommendations:
10.6 	We recommended that the City Accountant, City Budget Officer and City DRRM Officer comply with the requirement of RA No. 10121 on the reversion of the unexpended DRRMF balance to the GF after the lapse of five years to be used for other social services of the City as identified by the local sanggunian.
10.7 	We also recommended that on the basis of LDRRMF Utilization Report, the City Accountant effect the necessary journal entries to revert the unexpended LDRRMF balance to the GF.

Recommendation:
11.12 	We reiterated our recommendation that the City Planning and Development Coordinator and City Budget Officer refrain from providing lump sum appropriations in the AB and AIP.  Instead, identify and indicate specific PPAs and allocate budget accordingly for each area of development/office with corresponding project cost including the necessary fund flows to approximate the reasonable timing in the release of funds and period of implementation of PPAs.

Recommendations:
12.6 	We recommended that the City Accountant:
Refrain from recording as receipts the use of equipment for infrastructure projects implemented by Administration as this practice runs counter to the IG No. 1 of IPSAS 9; and
Analyze the transactions and effect the necessary journal entries to restate the correct amount of Rental Income in the books of accounts of the Motorpool Operation.

Recommendations:
13.8	We recommended that:
The LSB provide a written explanation as to the cause of delay and/or non-implementation of the above discussed PPAs; and
The OCE submit Status of Implementation of PPAs and to continuously fast-track and monitor the implementation of on-going and unimplemented projects.

Recommendations:
14.10 	We recommended that Management:
Strictly adhere to Sections 219 and 221 of the LGC for future revisions of fair market values of real properties in the City and assessments thereof;
Direct the Office of the City Assessor and the OCT-Land Tax Division to test all the program features of the newly installed system and submit a detailed report of inconsistencies for Management to communicate with the service provider to further enhance the system and generate accurate data, documents and reports; and
Direct the Office of the City Assessor to comply with Section 5, Article XI of Ordinance No. 18-286 to furnish the OCT of the copy of the Idle Land Properties of the City.

Recommendation:
15.6 	We recommended that the City Accountant facilitate the submission of all paid DVs, LRs and their supporting documents to the Auditor and to strictly comply with the prescribed period within which to submit the financial and transaction documents under the aforementioned Circulars.

Recommendation:
16.14 	We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant and BAC to immediately submit to COA the lacking documents as itemized in Appendix A for verification and henceforth, ensure that all documentary requirements for emergency purchase under the above mentioned and succeeding rules and regulations of the GPPB are complied with prior to payment to ensure the legality, propriety and validity of the claims and completeness of supporting documents for each claim or disbursement.

Recommendations:
17.8 	We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City Engineer and City Planning and Development Coordinator to integrate the CSHP in the computation of construction costs of all projects to be implemented by the City, whether by contract or by Administration, to ensure the protection and welfare of the workers at the construction site as well as the general public passing around the construction sites; and
Bids and Awards Committee to require interested Contactors to submit DOLE-approved CSHP to ensure the latter’s compliance with Occupational Safety and Health Standards set by the DOLE.

Recommendation:
18.9	We recommended that Management direct the:
City Engineer (CE) to submit to COA a written explanation on the deficiencies noted above and intensify efforts by installing additional written policies and procedures in the control and monitoring of ERROs; and
CE and Motorpool In-charge to conduct monthly reconciliation of ERROs for the OCA to arrive at an accurate project costs apportioned for equipment use at year-end.

Recommendations:
20.18	We recommended that Management:
On the improvement of planning and budgeting capabilities:
Carefully plan and review PPAs to be included in the AIP and as much as possible identify cost of each program/project and avoid lump sum allocation; and
Prepare APPs that are duly linked to AIP to ensure that every procurement is cost efficient and effective;
On unimplemented projects:
Provide timeline for every program/project and make sure that the timelines set are realistic and are strictly followed;
Fast track the implementation of those PPAs which have not yet been started and have remained unfinished as at year-end;
Craft detailed project monitoring and evaluation guidelines to ensure accountability and completion of PPAs within the timelines set and also to institutionalize best practices and interventions for infrastructure and non-infrastructure projects.
On delay and deficiencies in the implementation of infrastructure projects by administration:
Require the OCE to conduct of feasibility study and detailed engineering before project implementation.
Support the POW with Technical Design Plans and Specifications
Install internal control or policies including monitoring procedures on the request of LGU equipment for use, rent or lease and monitoring of the status of implementation of infrastructure projects by the project engineers.

Recommendations:
21.12 We recommended that management:
Require the City Accountant (CA) in coordination with the OIC of the HRMDO to extensively review/analyze the accounts with negative balances in the aggregate amount of ₱9,147,052.32 million and effect proper adjustments for fair presentation of the accounts in the FS.
Direct CA and OIC-HRMDO to exert more effort to ensure that all withheld contributions are remitted intact and on a regular basis and reconcile on a monthly basis their respective records to prevent occurrence of negative balances and reflect the correct balances in the books of accounts.

Recommendations:
22.9 	We recommended that Management direct the CA and OIC-HRMDO to:
Submit written explanation as to the reason why withheld HDMF and PHIC contributions cannot be remitted in full which resulted to the increase of the same accounts as of the year ended 2020;
Coordinate with HDMF and PHIC agencies to resolve these unremitted contributions and remit such balances; and
Continuously exert more effort to ensure that all withheld contributions are remitted intact and on a regular basis.

Recommendation:
23.7 	We recommended that the OCT and OCA to continuously exert more efforts in ensuring that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and on a regular basis.

Recommendation:
24.6 	We recommended that Management direct the GAD FPS to submit to the Auditor the revised DILG-endorsed and agency-approved GPB for CY 2020 and GAD AR and to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to COA.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OCE and all concerned officials to submit all perfected government contracts within five days from execution, complete with all supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and require all concerned officials to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that management direct:

The City Treasurer to remit the outstanding balance of the 5 per cent of the DPWH of ₱439,669.74 to the BTr as indicated in the above table; and

The Building Official to prepare a Program of Work for approval by the LCE and to submit the same to the DPWH Secretary thru the NBCDO for the utilization of 15 per cent share in accordance with the procedural guidelines set under DILG-DPWH JMC No. 001.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct all concerned officials who prepared the ABC to submit justification/explanation with supporting documents on the variance noted above for further evaluation by the COA RTS, otherwise, the variance of ₱295,128.94 is to be disallowed in audit, if unjustified, as this condition is unfavorable to the government per COA Resolution No. 2015-014.

For future related transactions, we also recommended that the City Engineer ensure that the preparation of ABC is compliant with the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airports, and other related laws, rules, and regulations to avoid any excess on the contract cost over COA cost estimate.

We recommended that the City Mayor require all concerned officials to revisit and    adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

It is also recommended that the City Engineer:

Submit to COA, at the beginning of each year, the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are for implementation during the year; 

Direct the City Engineer to inform COA that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices have already been posted within the prescribed period for validation; and

Fully update all data in their Monthly Monitoring Report to keep track of the progress of all PPAs within his power/jurisdiction from planning stage to implementation to completion since it is incumbent upon him as City Engineer of the LGU to “administer, coordinate, supervise, and control the construction, maintenance, improvement, repair of roads, bridges, and other engineering and public works projects of the local government unit concerned” pursuant to Section 477 of RA 7160.  Also, the CPDO shall be furnished with the said report as input to the required report of COA.

We recommended that management direct the Acting CGSO and Project Engineer/s to closely monitor all projects implemented by administration to ensure its completion within the time specified in the program of work. Any delay in the completion thereof is to be supported with necessary documents and strictly observed the provisions of Item No. 3 of COA Circular No. 80-101-C dated July 28, 1980.

We recommended that management resolve issues encountered in the proposed sites of the MRF, fast-track the implementation and turn-over of the remaining MRFs to its barangays and continuously monitor barangays’ compliance with the SWM Law on the establishment of MRF.

We reiterated our recommendation that management direct:

The City Assessor’s Office to initiate and coordinate with all other involved offices to fast track the implementation of the revised Schedule of Fair Market Values per City Ordinance No. 18-286; and

The City Assessor’s Office to include in their CY 2020 schedule the activities necessary for the complete identification of idle lands to allow prompt implementation of the additional provision; and

The Offices of the City Treasurer and City Assessor to coordinate with their personnel regarding their concerns on the existing electronic system and to bring these up with the computer specialist or programmer in order to address these issues.

We recommended that the AOs be provided with fully functional and secured drawers with locks to store his accountable forms and records for safekeeping to minimize the risks of loss and to further strengthen controls in the safeguarding government assets.

We recommended that management direct the City Treasurer to strictly monitor the effectivity and expiry dates of fidelity bonds of the City’s AOs and ensure, through the issuance of office memorandum, that applications of renewal of bond be made a month prior to their expiry dates.  No AO is to be allowed to perform their collecting and disbursing functions with expired bonds.

We recommended that the OCA to regularly monitor the movement of the CAs of certain AOs in order to restrict granting of additional CAs to those with existing balances, as well as to better oversee the timely liquidation of the same
We recommended that management to direct the OCE and CPDO to fast track the implementation of the SWM facilities/structures as envisioned in the SWMP of the City;

We recommended that the CGSO conduct physical count of all inventories held by the City, submit a properly accomplished RPCI by type for the succeeding semesters, and coordinate with the City Accountant for the reconciliation and adjustment of the related inventory accounts.

We recommended that the OCE review the documents related to, and to prepare covering Project Accomplishment Reports, and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance for the completed projects, and to submit the same to the OCA to serve as basis to take up journal entries transferring completed projects from the CIP to the appropriate asset / Public Infrastructure accounts and their corresponding registries to correctly present the assets and equity accounts of the City.

We recommended that more effort be exerted to fully implement planned GAD programs and activities for the year in accordance with DBM, NEDA, National Commission on the Role Filipino Women JC No. 2004-1.
We recommended that management revisit and strictly observe the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, or the Government of Procurement Reform Act on the hiring of consultants.

We recommended that the agency ensure that procurement of common use supplies shall be made from the PS, in compliance with RA 9184, AO No. 17 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6.  This way, the agency is one with the government in its support of transparency, competitiveness, streamlined procurement process, system of accountability, and public monitoring.

Submit a written explanation on the discrepancy in the totals reflected in the Construction and Heavy Equipment account; and
Proceed with the necessary actions for the identified shortage in the Military, Police and Security Equipment and to report the same to the OCA to make the adjustments, when necessary, in the books of accounts; and
Maintain Property Cards for all types of PPE, especially in the recording of properties at actual cost in order to match with the records of the OCA.

Direct the OIC-HRMDO to coordinate with PhilHealth and Pag-Ibig agencies to resolve these unremitted contributions and remit such balances, if warranted; and
Direct the City Accountant and OIC-HRMDO to exert more efforts to ensure that all withheld contributions are remitted intact and on a regular basis and reconcile on a monthly basis their respective records to prevent occurrence of negative balances and reflect the correct balances in the books of accounts.

Fast track the implementation of those PPAs which have not yet started and have remained unfinished as at year-end; and
Exert more efforts in their project monitoring system and   consistently observe and enhance the same so that PPAs are completed on time, hence, resulting to prompt delivery of socio-economic benefits to its constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the CAO look into the RPCPPE prepared by the AMT and take necessary actions to reflect the results of the reconciliation efforts in the books of accounts of the City.
We also recommended that the CGSO maintain Property Cards for all types of PPE, especially in the recording of properties at actual cost in order to match with the records of the CAO. The CGSO in turn should reflect in the RPCPPE the property ID numbers to match with all the property records of the City.

We recommended that the City Accountant designate one of her staff to prepare and maintain proper LRNLCs to support the figures reflected in the financial reports.
Further, we recommended that the CGSO incorporate in their property records the cost figures for all RN components as required in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Lastly, we recommended that management direct the CEO, CAO, and CGSO to take the necessary actions for the City’s compliance on the requirements set forth in the COA Circular No. 2015-008 regarding the City’s LRN Systems, especially in the identification of costs or value to each of the RN components.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the CAO and CGSO to prioritize the reconciliation of the results of the physical count of inventories or RCPI with their respective records and immediately investigate, clear and reconcile all discrepancies between physical count and accounting and property records. If necessary, written explanations shall be required from accountable and responsible persons.
Require the City Accountant to:
1.  Conduct review, analysis and reconciliation of the dormant accounts and determine their existence and validity.  Effect the necessary journal entries, if warranted;
2.   Demand from persons responsible the submission of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of the RIS as basis in the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reflect the correct balances of the FOL and Drugs and Medicines Inventory accounts in the books; and
3.    Disclose all unreconciled accounts in the Notes to the FS.

We recommended that management direct the CAO and CTO to exert more effort in tracing the origin, and determining the nature/status of the accounts with no known account numbers, and taking the necessary action to properly reflect the true condition of the ₱2,062,059.46 CIB accounts in the books of the City.

We recommended that Management direct the CTO to:
Discontinue placing funds from SEF and TF in time deposits totaling ₱67,000,047.33 and transfer said funds to their respective current accounts so they can readily be available for use in the implementation of planned PPAs of the City;
Retrieve copies of the SP Resolutions for the opening of the time deposits accounts in the GF totaling ₱18,000,000.00 and that these be kept as permanent files in the CTO, together with all other SP resolutions, communications, and any similar document regarding the addition of funds or termination of the said accounts, and to
Furnish copies of the SP resolution authorizing the opening of the time deposits to the Auditor’s Office upon retrieval. Should these be unavailable, we recommended that the CTO should communicate with the corresponding banks for file copies of any similar documents.

We recommended that Management:
Require the CPDO, CEO and Heads of Implementing Offices of BuB Program to explain to COA why projects were not completed within the timeframe; and
Fast track the implementation of the identified projects and henceforth, intensify proper planning and timely execution of PPAs, as well as continuous monitoring and evaluation of the same to address issues that may be needed to be resolved during implementation.

We recommended that Management direct:
The CPDO and CEO to fast track the implementation of the construction and repair of school buildings and other structures;
The LSB to stop the practice of lump-sum budgeting of PPAs and evaluate the PPAs to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth by the provisions of RA 7160, RA 5447 and Section 7 (b) of RA 10410 on the inclusion of PPAs for ECCD; and
The City Budget and Accounting Offices to correct the noted errors in its reports, ensure that all DVs and transaction documents provide details of the PPAs being implemented and assist the LSB in the evaluation and proper classification of PPAs into major account expenditures (e.g. PS, MOOE and CO).

We recommended that the City Mayor direct all concerned officials to submit all POs and contracts within five days from issuance, complete with all supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001, and for the CGSO to notify the Auditor of the deliveries within 24 hours upon delivery.

We recommended that management:
Direct the CEO and CPDO to fast track the implementation of the SWM facilities/structures as envisioned in the SWMP of the City;
Request CENRO to exert more efforts to control and properly dispose the bulks of garbage/wastes at the dumpsites to avert potential health and environment issues and provide enclosures/fence to restrict livestock from scavenging piled up sacks of wastes/garbage; and
Direct the City Budget Officer and City Accountant to coordinate and reconcile the variances found on their records, and adjust these accordingly.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant and City Treasurer to review their respective records pertaining to the above accounts, investigate and reconcile noted differences between their records, adjust the accounts, if warranted and remit immediately to the BTr the unexpended balances of PDAF in compliance with the Supreme Court decisions.

We recommended that management:
a. 	Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all AFs in the CTO to reconcile discrepancies noted in the CRAAF and the individual record book per AF, and, prepare RPCI for each type of AF to be submitted to the COA for review/verification.
b. 	Implement safeguards in the access to AF. Always lock the door in the storage area if not being used or accessed by authorized personnel to avoid possible loss or misuse of these items.
c. 	Require the assistant property custodians, under the close supervision of the AO and the City Treasurer, to inspect all unused AF caused by spoilage, deterioration and/or obsolescence in the storage area and transfer these AFs to a separate but secured store room temporarily to accommodate new stocks of AF. Once these unused AFs are cleared from the store room, new stocks of AF from the boxes should be properly piled and labeled in their designated shelves.  Each stub should be numbered in sequence, especially those forms that do not have inclusive serial numbers like cash tickets, to intensify control over the receipt, issuance and reporting of AF.
d. 	Initiate the disposal of the obsolete AF pursuant to Section 99 of the GAAM.

We recommended that Management thru the City Treasurer (CT) direct the AOs to maintain their own cashbooks and record thereat all receipts and disbursements under their accountability, and to brief or train the AOs on the procedures on how to accomplish and maintain cashbooks.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant in coordination with the OIC of the Human Resource Management and Development Office (HRMDO) to review/analyze the accounts with negative balances in the aggregate amount of P7,985,569.69 and effect proper adjustments, if warranted, for fair presentation of the accounts in the FS.
We also recommended that the City Accountant and OIC-HRMDO exert more effort to ensure that all withheld contributions are remitted intact and on a regular basis, reconcile on a monthly basis their respective records to prevent occurrence of negative balances and reflect the correct balances in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to extensively review/analyze the accounts with variances in the aggregate amount of P3,385,160.36 and effect proper adjustments, if warranted, for fair presentation of the Due to BIR account in the FS.
It is also recommended that the City Treasurer exert more efforts to ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and within the prescribed period.

Future submission of the RPCPPE be made with complete details. Each type of PPE should at least have a separate report and that entries in each column of the form prescribed in Volume II of the Manual for NGAS for LGUs should be present, especially the Cost and the Remarks column. 

The CGSO coordinate with the Accounting Office regarding the results of the physical count to determine the PPE accounts that require reconciliation. 


Property/ledger cards be maintained by the General Services and Accounting Offices, which should be reconciled with the annual physical inventory conducted. Discrepancies noted should be immediately verified and adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management to require strict adherence to the terms and conditions set forth in the POs issued. For projects undertaken by Administration, build temporary warehouses or bodegas in the jobsites, secure these accordingly if the existing warehouses of the City cannot accommodate all the purchased materials, and seriously consider upgrading its warehouses or bodegas where to keep construction materials purchased. Keeping these with suppliers is not a sound internal control practice, and gives undue advantage to suppliers who get control over the stocks in their possession. Considering that the City governs a fast growing 2nd class city, whose operations reflect substantial income, there is no reason why it could not have warehouses built for storing or keeping and securing construction materials purchased.  It should level up to the demands for proper accountability.
