observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:


    We recommended that management require the CGSO to:


   a. Submit RPCPPEs with complete details. Each type of PPE should  have  a separate report with the columns of the form prescribed in  Volume  II of the Manual for NGAS property filled-up, especially the Cost and the Remarks columns.


    b.      Coordinate with the Accounting Section on the reconciliation  of the results of the physical count with the book balances  of  the  PPE accounts,  with  the  reconciliation  to  be  done  immediately   upon identification of the unreconciled accounts.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that the:


   a. Accounting Section and  the  CGSO,  or  the  concerned  personnel  who control  the  release  and  liquidation  of  diesel  and  gasoline  to reconcile their  records  and  to  speed  up  the  preparation  and/or submission of liquidation documents, if any.


   b. Personnel concerned to explain the huge discrepancy between  the  fuel inventory  and  the   book   balance   and   to   trace/dig   up   the documents/records of the previous years, if necessary.

Recommendation:


    We recommended that the Accounting Office review and reconcile  the  two reciprocal  accounts  and  effect  the  necessary  adjustments.   Reciprocal accounts should be regularly reconciled to arrive  at  the  same  reconciled balances as of a given period since differences between these  accounts  may mean overstatements and/or understatements of  affected  accounts  presented in the financial statements.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that the:


   a. CGSO conduct a physical count of all inventories  held  by  the  City, submit a properly accomplished RPCI for  the  second  semester  of  CY  2016, and coordinate with the City Accountant for  the  reconciliation and adjustment of  the  related  inventory  accounts,  and  to  ensure compliance by the property custodians on the submission of  SSMIs  for consolidation and submission to the Accounting  Office  to  facilitate the recording of expenses.


   b. Accounting Office comply with the provision of Section 121 of Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs regarding the  recording  of  expenditures based on the submitted SSMIs by the CGSO.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that the City Accountant exert more effort  in  requiring the  liquidation  of  cash  advances  by  sending  demand  letters  to   the accountable officers concerned  and  effect  the  necessary  sanctions  upon failure to liquidate the  same,  and  to  prepare  the  necessary  adjusting entries, where appropriate, regarding  the  existing  negative  balances  of some accountable officers.


    We also recommended that the concerned personnel in charge of  the  pre- audit of cash advances regularly monitor the movement of the  cash  advances of certain accountable officers in order to facilitate  the  restriction  on granting additional cash advances as well as to better  oversee  the  timely liquidation of the same.

Recommendation:


    We recommended that management require the City  Treasurer’s  Office  to submit a written explanation as to  the  whereabouts  of  the documents of those unrecorded disbursements, submit the related vouchers  and  supporting papers to the Accounting Office and request for  /  secure  from  depository banks for copies of unrecorded debit and credit memoranda of prior years  or any other document that could facilitate the adjustment of  the  said  items in the books.


    We also recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Offices exert  more effort in obtaining the necessary documents to effect the adjustment of the remaining unreconciled items.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that management be guided by Item Nos. 1 and  2  of  PHIC Circular No. 16 with regards to the management of the PCF and  charges  that could be made from the said Fund. It could come out with guidelines  on  how disbursements from the PCF are to be authorized and processed.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that the  Planning,  Engineering,  Treasury,  Accounting, CSWD and other concerned divisions/offices coordinate efforts to fast  track the  implementation  of  BuB  projects  to  benefit  the  City’s  grassroots population.


       We also recommended that management require the BuB focal  person  to regularly monitor the status of proposed BuB  projects  as  to  funding  and implementation.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that in the preparation of the POWs,  over  estimates  of quantities of materials  and/or  labor  needed  in  the  project  should  be avoided. In cases where  adjustments/changes  are  to  be  made  during  the course of the implementation of the project, a variation or a  change  order will have to be prepared.


        We also recommended that the personnel in charge of the  preparation of POWs, plans and specifications be made to submit an explanation  for the excess estimate.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that management  appropriate  and  disburse  the  20%  DF strictly for investment and capital expenditures only,  in  accordance  with Section 2.3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 and include projects of the  kind  as those enumerated in Section 3 of the same JMC.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that management direct  the  LDRRMO  to  include  in  the LDRRMFIP the programs, projects and activities  to  be  funded  out  of  the unexpended LDRRMF  of  CYs  2011-2014,  and  thereafter  to  ensure  maximum benefit from the Fund.

Recommendation:


    We recommended that management submit the breakdown of expenses together with the related invoices and official receipts pertaining to  the  activity required per Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, and to support  further activities and expenditures of similar nature  with  complete  documentation pursuant to the cited provisions. It was also recommended that EME  be  made  within the allowable limits mandated by Section 325 (h) of RA 7160.

Recommendation:

       We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation  to  discourage  the practice of granting cash advances to DepEd employees  for  the  purpose  of defraying expenses related to sport activities. Instead, cash  advances  for the conduct of athletic meets chargeable against the SEF should  be  granted to the regular accountable officers of the City  especially  so  that  these activities entail considerable amounts of cash outlay.

        We  also  recommended  that  the  liquidation  of  the  unliquidated previous year’s cash advances be followed up  and  that  demand  letters  be issued, when necessary.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that management should refrain from charging expenses not covered under Section 272 of RA 7160 to the SEF, and for all  claims  to  be supported with complete documentation as required  under  Section  4  of  PD 1445.

Recommendation:

    We reiterated our previous recommendation that  management  provide  the concerned offices with sufficient personnel who can  efficiently  carry  out the delegated duties. Also, coordination amongst offices  to  set  deadlines regarding submission of their respective reports  and/or  documents  to  the  Accounting Office would facilitate early preparation of reports.


    We also recommended that the  CGSO  conduct  a  physical  count  of  all inventories of the City for the second  semester  of  this  year  and  every period thereafter, and submit properly accomplished  reports  in  compliance with Section 124 of Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that the:


   a. CENRO to coordinate and work with the garbage and disposal  committees of the barangays and together study and evaluate how these  provisions on Waste Segregation at Source can be  fully  implemented.  The  CENRO must designate barangay personnel to look into the proper  segregation of  waste  and  require  the  assistance  of  the  barangay  for   the dissemination of the policy  of  no  segregation,  no  collection  and impose certain guidelines for not complying with the law.


   b. Barangay Captains to comply with the provision on the establishment of MRFs and extend logistical assistance to them, if necessary.

Recommendation:

    We  recommended  that  a  fully-functional  GFPS  be   established   and strengthened so that GAD activities can be mainstreamed  into  the  projects and programs of the City as required by law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the concerned officials from the City General Services Office (CGSO) and the Accounting Section reconcile the result of the PPE physical inventory with the balances in the books of accounts to ensure the accuracy and validity of the balances reflected in the financial statements of the City.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Staff will be assigned to take charge of the reconciliation of the LGU’s PPE. Personnel involved in the process will undergo basic training/updates on property management.
observations.reason: Physical inventory taking of Inventories and PPE was conducted resulting from a recommendation on of the CY2014 audit. However, balances per inventory reports did not tally with book balances. Due to the conversion of the accounts of the LGU effective January 1, 2015 transactions, there was no Accounting Staff assigned to focus on the reconciliation of the PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer and City Accountant be required to reconcile their cash in bank records on a monthly basis, and for the latter to effect corrections or adjustments as necessary to clear the reconciling items appearing in the BRS. The accountable officers concerned should submit supporting documents such as validated deposit slips for unrecorded deposits, paid vouchers for unrecorded disbursements, debit / credit memo and other necessary documents which would be the basis for adjustments made.
observations.managementAction: A total of P13,738,659.15 bank reconciling items were adjusted.
observations.reason: Reconciling items date back to as early as 1995 and will require someone to look into available records. Absence of details of the reconciling items makes reconciliation hard. The Accounting personnel are still making adjustments in the books relative to PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that complete details of the reconciling items be indicated to facilitate verification/reconciliation. We recommended that efforts should be exerted to trace and verify the whereabouts of this account. The Treasury Section should be required to look for the source documents of this money to determine whether it still exists or not.
observations.managementAction: Collection letters were again sent out starting 2nd qtr of 2016 which resulted to a significant reduction in the unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Tracing of transactions was temporarily stopped to give way to the conversion of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant exert more effort in requiring the liquidation of cash advances by sending demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and effect the necessary sanctions upon failure to liquidate the same.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, as of December 31, 2016, a total of 88 unliquidated cash advances were settled amounting to P7,881,566.31.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe strict adherence with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2009-002 which limits the granting of cash advances only for a legally authorized specific purpose. The cash advance voucher should indicate with clarity, the specific purpose/nature of the expense the cash advance is drawn. Support with a duly approved estimate of expenses the cash advance for non-recurring expenses granted to special disbursing officers.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was initiated but not finished. Support with a duly approved estimate of expenses for partial compliance will be submitted after the closing of the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Reconciliation was initiated but not finished; temporarily stopped due to year-end reports. Accounting staff will start complying with COA Cir. 2016-005 in March 2017, since it is now busy with the year-end reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB be guided by the provisions of RA 7160 and the Dep-Ed-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions thereof shall be submitted to the Dep-Ed for resolution and in consultation with the City Government. It should give priority to projects that are beneficial to the constituents of the City such as repair of school buildings, facilities and etc.
observations.managementAction: Management will confer as to what will be the best action to take on this without violating any existing laws, rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB be guided by the provisions and discourage the practice of granting cash advances to DepEd employees for the purpose of defraying expenses related to sport activities. Cash advances for the conduct of athletic meets chargeable against the SEF should be granted to the regular accountable officers of the City especially so that these activities entail considerable amounts of cash outlay.
observations.managementAction: Management has implemented: Effective January, 2016, SFP cash advances were supported with approved estimates of expenses. Liquidations were facilitated and the FUR on SFP was updated and on-time. Unexpended balance of P81,420.28 was returned to the DSWD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City consider opening a separate account for DSWD funds for purposes of reflecting in the books and in the bank statements, the accurate bank balance of the funds for supplemental feeding and at the same time to facilitate verification of Statement of Receipts and Disbursements (SORDs) and Fund Utilization Report (FURs). The Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) to maintain a record of cash accountability thru the use of the cashbook for this program, and to downsize the voluminous listings and repetitive details of report of disbursements for each of the day care centers.
observations.managementAction: Effective January, 2016, RPT and SET receivables were set up in the books. Liquidation reports were updated and on-time. A separate bank account for SFP was opened with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) Branch Dipolog last April 2016. Unexpended balance of P81,420.28 was returned to the DSWD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer and the City Accountant shall maintain stock cards and supplies ledger cards for construction materials in stock. The City should return unutilized PDAF to the National Treasury and downsize the voluminous listings and repetitive details of report of disbursements for each of the day care centers by incorporating, in summary form, the needed information on the actual utilization of the cash advance.

We recommended that the City Accounting Department facilitate the analysis of the accounts per subsidiary ledger and revert immediately the balance to the National Treasury in compliance with the decision of the Supreme Court. We recommended that the City Accountant require the submission of the list of taxpayers with the tax due as basis for the setting up of the receivables and not base the journal entry on mere letter of communication from the City Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Mandatory reports during the 1st half of CY 2016 were delayed, however, by year end, these reports were submitted within the required time frame in compliance with the Full Disclosure Policy.
observations.reason: The LGU is dependent on the billboard required by DPWH which has a different format from that required by COA. The City Accountant’s Office had some lapses on its compliance with the provisions of PD 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Treasurer to furnish the City Accountant a copy of the certified list of taxpayers and the amount of tax due at the beginning of every year to be used as basis for the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivables to avoid any abnormal balances of said accounts, and for the City Accountant to require submission of the list of taxpayers with the tax due as basis for the setting up of the receivables and not base the journal entry on mere letter of communication from the City Treasurer. We also recommended that the concerned offices be required to implement control measures necessary for the timely transmittal of documents to prepare reports earlier and for the Accounting and Treasury Sections be provided with sufficient number of personnel to efficiently carry out the delegated duties.
observations.managementAction: Three out of the four projects were implemented in early 2016. Management is trying to locate the titles and the acquisition costs of the recorded Land account. The City Treasurer furnished the City Accountant, a copy of the certified list of taxpayers and the amount of tax due at the beginning of every year to be used as basis for the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivables to avoid abnormal balances.
observations.reason: Some parts of the AIP of CY 2016 were still in generic terms. Problems encountered hindered the proper implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the requirements embodied in COA Circular No. 2013-004 be fully and immediately complied with to promote transparency and accountability and to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern, and to avoid the penalty clause stated therein. We recommended that the Accounting Office be provided with sufficient personnel and that the concerned offices implement control measures necessary for the timely transmittal of documents to prepare reports earlier.
observations.managementAction: The City already had an approved and recently revised its 10 year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) where a SLF is included. Inventory reports have been regularly submitted to COA. The City is in compliance with submission of required reports though some were delayed due to conversion to PPSAS.
observations.reason: The focus on the recent PPSAS conversion led to the late preparation of the CY 2016 financial reports, causing lengthy delays on the submission of monthly financial reports, bank reconciliation statements, as well as vouchers and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned offices be required to implement control measures necessary for the timely transmittal of documents to prepare reports earlier and for the Accounting and Treasury Sections be provided with sufficient number of personnel to efficiently carry out the delegated duties.
observations.managementAction: Schedules have been set primarily for the orientation/education of the members of the Technical Working Group to empower and enable them to implement the City’s GAD program. Inventory reports have been regularly submitted to COA.
observations.reason: The placing of garbage containers on strategic places of the City did not work because some were stolen and non-residents of Dipolog City also placed their garbage in said containers. This is reiterated in AO # 17, Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management: On Waste Segregation at Source: The City must provide a properly marked or identified containers for on-site collection as: “compostable”, “recyclable” or “special waste”, or any other classification as maybe needed. The Office of the City Environmental and Natural Resources (OCENR) must coordinate and work with the garbage and disposal committees of the barangays and together study and evaluate on how these provisions on Waste Segregation at Source can be fully implemented; and to designate barangay personnel to look into the proper segregation of waste and require the assistance of the barangay for the dissemination of the policy of no segregation, no collection and impose sanctions for not complying with the law. On the establishment of a SLF, the City should prepare and plan for the establishment of a SLF as required by RA 9003. On the promulgation of Ordinances: The City must pass an ordinance on the full implementation of the SWMP and the SLF.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that there are 18 existing MRFs around the City but most of these are non-functional. The CENRO regularly visited the barangay captains, the Chairman of the Barangay Solid Waste Management committees and discussed ways to segregate waste efficiently and to reactivate the non-functional MRFs.
observations.reason: Lack of training of the concerned personnel and staff on the implementation of the GAD Program of the LGU resulted to confusion and misinterpretation of the guidelines set. Bodegas and warehouses were still not constructed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Environmental and Natural Resources Office (OCENR) to coordinate and work with the garbage and disposal committees of the barangays and together study and evaluate on how these provisions on Waste Segregation at Source can be fully implemented; and The CENRO must designate barangay personnel to look into the proper segregation of waste and require the assistance of the barangay for the dissemination of the policy of no segregation, no collection and impose sanctions for not complying with the law.

We recommended that management require strict adherence to the terms and conditions set forth in the POs issued; For projects undertaken by Administration, build temporary warehouses or bodegas in the jobsites, and secure these accordingly if the existing warehouses of the City cannot accommodate all the purchased materials.
observations.managementAction: Inventory reports have been regularly submitted to COA.
observations.reason: The placing of garbage containers on strategic places of the City did not work because some were stolen and non-residents of Dipolog City also placed their garbage in said containers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC be instructed to secure historical data related to the original acquisition of the delivered reconditioned transport and heavy equipment and compute the Remaining Useful Value using the formula provide in NBC 446-A, as amended, to determine that the price actually paid did not exceed the Remaining Useful Value (RUV).
observations.managementAction: Considering that the City governs a fast growing 2nd class city, whose operations reflect substantial income, there is no reason why it could not have warehouses built for storing or keeping construction materials purchased. Keeping these with suppliers is not a sound internal control practice, and gives undue advantage to suppliers who get control over the stocks in their possession.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government, in coordination with the legislative proponent of the PDAF to secure authority, if possible, from DBM on the change of the purpose of the fund from “Social Services: food, transportation, medical, burial and education assistance of the City of Dipolog” to “Purchase of Motor Vehicle” of the DepEd Division of the Zamboanga del Norte. Likewise, seek authority or post-facto approval from the Secretary of DILG for the acquired motor vehicle. We reiterate our recommendation that the management require: City Accountant to record all regular purchases of supplies and materials on stock through the proper inventory account and take up the corresponding issuances as expense on the basis of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be prepared and submitted by the City General Services Office (CGSO) weekly, as means of ensuring the accuracy of the inventory account. Property Custodian of the various offices to prepare and submit the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and SSMI to the CGSO for consolidation.
observations.managementAction: Inventory reports have been regularly submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and related documents.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that the: CGSO and the Assessor’s Office reconcile their list of properties and that the former furnish the latter with photocopies of the duplicate title of properties registered in the name of the City Government of Dipolog. The CGSO account for duplicate of titles not in her possession of which only photocopies were on file. The CGSO and the City Accountant prepare Property Card and Real Property Ledger Card, respectively for each property. City Accountant determine the composition of the balance of the land account in 2002 totalling P18,546,484.83 and prepare corresponding Real Property Ledger Card for each identified lot. Likewise, it is recommended that those properties with deed of sale and deed of donation which are not yet titled be processed for titling so that the ownership of the land can be transferred in the name of the City Government of Dipolog.
observations.managementAction: Management has been trying to locate the titles and the acquisition costs of the recorded Land account. The CGSO account for duplicate of titles not in her possession of which only photocopies were on file. Management maintained its stand that the consultancy services involved are not those subject to the provisions of RA 9184, as these are subject to the discretion of the appointing authority where services are based on trust and confidence.
observations.reason: Management still failed to fully comply with the recommendation but under GPPB Opinion No. NPM 032-2005 dated July 18, 2005, a bidding process in accordance with Section 8.2.1 of the IRR of RA 9184 will still have to be undertaken in hiring consultants.
